task_metadata.current_task: Implement chunked file reading for hash computation (8KB chunks)

nodes.description: Choose hashlib for computing file content hashes
nodes.rationale: hashlib is a standard library module in Python that provides secure hash algorithms (SHA256, MD5, etc.). It's reliable, built-in, and doesn't require external dependencies. SHA256 is preferred for collision resistance and security.
nodes.task_state: Initialized core hashing strategy for content deduplication

nodes.rationale: os.walk() is a standard library function that recursively traverses directory trees, handling both files and subdirectories automatically. It's efficient and provides file names and directory structure without external dependencies.
dependencies: os module - provides os.walk() for directory traversal

nodes.description: Large files must be processed in chunks to avoid loading entire content into memory
nodes.rationale: Files can be arbitrarily large (GBs or TBs). Loading entire files into memory would cause out-of-memory errors. Chunked reading with hash streaming is the solution.

nodes.description: Implement chunked file reading for hash computation (8KB chunks)
nodes.rationale: Reading files in fixed-size chunks (8KB) allows hashing of arbitrarily large files without loading them entirely into memory. This mitigates the memory constraint and is efficient for I/O performance.
dependencies: hashlib.sha256 - supports incremental hash updates via update() method

edges.rationale: Directory walking must occur before content hashing; files discovered by os.walk() are then hashed using hashlib
Chunked reading decision was directly shaped by the memory constraint requirement to handle large files
